decisions: Initialize .context so project-specific coding and workflow rules are shared in the repository.
Document .context entry points in AGENTS.md so future agents read prefs before modifying code.
risk: Low; adds repository workflow metadata and does not change site runtime behavior.

decisions: Reuse FlowExperimentCanvas for the agent-loop scene so every animation lab experiment follows the same canvas contract.
Split large motion CSS into scene and keyframe files to keep animation-lab component files under the 500-line project limit.
Enhance check-animation-lab with node/path/packet reference validation and file length enforcement.
Accumulate trace events across steps so the trace panel shows completed prior events while preserving the active step.
risk: Medium; shared animation-lab canvas, trace display, and validation script behavior changed together, covered by targeted checks and site build.

decisions: Add 8 workflow-focused animation lab experiments for planning queues, patch application, test repair, prompt assembly, sub-agent merging, browser verification, safety filtering, and delivery review.
Keep AnimationLabIndex on a single data-driven FlowExperimentCanvas path instead of restoring per-experiment wrapper components or adding an in-page experiment menu.
Extend the canvas contract with packet arrays, node states, badges, and annotations so richer scenes stay declarative in data files.
Update sidebar anchors, docs metadata, and check-animation-lab so the 19-experiment catalog remains synchronized and validates packet/annotation node references.
Merged session note: 按二期计划新增 8 个 animation-lab 数据驱动实验，复用 FlowExperimentCanvas 和现有播放器。
risk: Medium; expands the shared animation-lab catalog and canvas rendering contract, with .qoder generated materials intentionally left outside the commit.

summary: feat(animation-lab): refine experiment motion and navigation
decisions: Add stream, validate, merge, and diff motion variants to make later animation-lab experiments visually distinct while keeping the shared FlowExperimentCanvas renderer.
Enrich the 19 experiment data files with denser step descriptions, additional trace events, annotations, and scene-specific motion choices instead of introducing per-experiment components.
Group the /animation-lab/ sidebar into five topic clusters so users can scan the lab by concept area without adding an in-page experiment menu.
Extend check-animation-lab to verify sidebar groups, all experiment hash entries, and the expanded motion vocabulary.
Merged session note: 将动画实验室侧边栏从单一长列表整理为 5 个主题分组，并在 check:animation-lab 中校验分组与 hash 入口。
risk: Medium; changes shared animation styles and every experiment data file, covered by targeted animation-lab checks, navigation checks, typecheck, and full site build.

decisions: Remove unreferenced legacy globally registered demo components and CSS animation variants from the VitePress theme runtime.
Delete matching unused composables and type contracts so the shared component type surface matches live components.
Keep historical planning and reference docs untouched because they are documentation assets, not runtime imports.
Merged session note: cleanup avoids deleting process docs; README refresh and .qoder ignore are committed separately.
risk: Medium; removes globally registered theme components, with references verified as non-runtime documentation-only matches.

decisions: Condense README from a full chapter inventory into current site entry points, content areas, local development commands, validation commands, and maintenance rules.
Point readers to navigation and docs directories for full chapter detail to reduce README drift as the VitePress site grows.
Capture current multi-area structure including discover, learning paths, animation lab, interview, and enterprise-agent content.
risk: Low; documentation-only update, but README no longer enumerates every chapter.

decisions: Add .qoder/ to .gitignore so local generated workspace files do not appear in repository status.
risk: Low; ignore-only change for a local tool directory.

summary: merge: sync origin/main after practice metadata updates
decisions: Resolve README conflict by keeping the concise project-overview structure from the local branch instead of restoring the long chapter inventory.
Preserve the incoming factual practice update by documenting that P14 has an additional MCP Server script, so P1-P28 maps to 29 TypeScript files.
Keep incoming practice metadata, learning-path checks, Trellis task records, and practice docs from origin/main as part of the pull merge.
risk: Medium; combines incoming practice metadata changes with local README restructuring, verified with targeted practice and TypeScript checks.

decisions: Extract tool calling, ReAct, retry, streaming, security, and session loop scenario data from Vue components into dedicated TypeScript modules so demos become data-driven and testable.
Move large scoped styles for practice demo components into sibling CSS files to keep Vue single-file components within the project file-size limit.
Add mode switches to existing practice demos instead of creating new page entries, preserving current documentation routes and component entry points.
Share security boundary scenario data between the P19 documentation page and the interactive demo to avoid duplicated rule/scenario definitions.
Merged session note: 在现有 ToolCallingLifecycle 组件中加入模式切换，并把流程数据拆到 toolCallingLifecycleScenario.ts。
Merged session note: 按 P10、P3、Session、P19、P4 的顺序实现动画模式增强，并统一采用 scenario 数据文件 + 组件模式切换。
Merged session note: 抽出 SecurityBoundary 的规则匹配函数，并改为大小写无关匹配。
risk: Medium; touches several globally registered VitePress practice demos and shared scenario data, with Git whitespace validation performed before commit.

summary: feat(animation-lab): add practice links to experiments
decisions: Add practiceLinks catalog metadata so each animation lab experiment points back to the closest practice lessons.
Render the selected experiment's related practice links below the existing motion player instead of moving or deleting chapter-level practice animations.
Extend check-animation-lab to require practice link coverage and validate linked practice pages exist.
Merged session note: 在动画实验室条目上增加关联实践入口，而不是迁移或删除 practice 章节内动画。
risk: Low; keeps practice routes and chapter components unchanged, with mapping drift guarded by animation-lab validation.

decisions: Fix the GraphRAG delayed visit state update with a small cancellable timer helper instead of a broader player abstraction.
Keep the stability-first scope selected by the user: add focus-visible states, button types, explicit transitions, and reduced-motion handling without changing practice page content or routes.
Merged session note: Implemented practice animation stability fixes without introducing a shared playback abstraction.
bugs: GraphRAG traversal scheduled a setTimeout that was not cleared by stop/reset, allowing stale callbacks to mutate node status after user interruption; replaced it with a cancellable visit timer and regression test.
risk: Low; targeted VitePress demo control and CSS changes, with build:strict still blocked by pre-existing TODO content outside this change.

decisions: Add a dedicated practice-motion index and check script so practice page motion coverage is tracked independently from animation-lab experiment data.
Reuse existing practiceProjects and animation-lab experiment identifiers instead of creating a parallel practice catalog.
Adjust release validation boundaries so archived and superpowers process docs are not treated as publishable unfinished content.
Add claude-code and hermes-agent to the learning content series contract because both series are present in site navigation.
Add missing chapter experience components and page entry points required by check:chapter-experience rather than weakening that validation.
Wire LearningProgressToggle into both chapter and practice guides so learning-progress validation covers both content surfaces.
Merged session note: 为 `/practice` 动效治理新增独立 practice-motion 索引与校验脚本，并接入 build:strict。
Merged session note: 修复 build:strict 阻塞时优先调整发布校验边界，并补齐已上导航课程系列和缺失章节体验入口。
risk: Medium; touches shared VitePress validation, global component registration, and chapter/practice guide surfaces, with build:strict passing before commit.

summary: docs(agents): establish agent operating manual and context system
decisions: Compress CLAUDE.md from a full project snapshot into a lean maintenance-rule index and add module-level CLAUDE.md indexes for components, data, and scripts linked from the root.

Establish an agent operating manual in AGENTS.md (Read First, default workflow, Do Not, high-risk plan gate) backed by a docs reference set: project-map, testing, dangerous-areas, debugging, and agent-plan-template.
Bump .claude/index.json scan to v4.2.0, register the generated module docs, and ignore the .agent/ harness scan output.
Merged session note: 将 CLAUDE.md 从详尽项目快照压缩为短维护规则，动态事实交给源码与校验脚本。
Merged session note: Initialize repository harness docs and make scripts/check.sh call the existing verified build:strict gate instead of guessed lint/test wrappers.
Merged session note: Replace TODO placeholders in docs/agent-plan-template.md with neutral instructional placeholders so check-content still passes.
risk: Low; documentation and harness tooling only, no site runtime or content-page change. Full build:strict gate not run in this commit session; whitespace and check:content verified.

summary: feat(content): guard code fence languages in check:content
decisions: Add a code fence language guard to check-content.mjs using a whitelist of allowed languages plus a state machine, so mistyped tags like diagram fail build:strict instead of silently falling back to plain text in Shiki.
Validate only top-level fences and strip VitePress modifiers ({1,2}, :line-numbers, [label]); display fences nested inside longer backtick blocks are treated as content rather than tags.
Choose a whitelist over a blacklist so any future wrong tag is caught, not only known-bad ones; register mermaid explicitly because vitepress-plugin-mermaid renders it rather than Shiki.
Fix 3 mislabeled diagram fences in interview/bagua/agent-basics to text, and sync scripts/CLAUDE.md with the new check responsibility.
risk: Low; build-time content validation plus one content file's fence tags only, no site runtime change. The whitelist initially omitted mermaid and was caught by the new check before commit; build:strict passes.

decisions: Update README to state build:strict now runs 13 validation scripts, including practice-motion governance.
Add the check:practice-motion command to the common checks table so the README matches the current validation pipeline.
risk: Low; README-only synchronization with existing validation scripts and no runtime behavior change.

decisions: Renumber agent-selection article files, public routes, sidebar entries, index links, and reading-map links into a continuous 01-24 sequence.
Add a theme-level client redirect map so old agent-selection numbered routes navigate to their new canonical routes while preserving query strings and hashes.
Compress cost and latency content from a broad selection article into a production budget check page, keeping deeper performance guidance delegated to /intermediate/32-performance-cost/.
Merged session note: 将 agent-selection frontmatter 中的缺失 recommendedNext 链接替换为现有相邻主题页面。
Merged session note: 保留 27/28/30，将生产相关分组改为“生产准入”，并把 29 收敛为“成本与延迟预算检查”。
Merged session note: 将 docs/agent-selection/ 文章文件名、公开路由、sidebar 编号和内部链接统一重排为连续 01-24。
risk: Medium; changes published agent-selection URLs and navigation. Old paths are covered by client redirects, but pure static hosting without SPA fallback still needs server-level redirect handling.

decisions: Add a demand-first decision tree to the agent-selection landing page so readers choose by task boundary before comparing frameworks or vendors.
Place the tree after the existing RAG/Search/Agent Framework model to turn the index from a link catalog into an actionable routing guide.
Keep the decision tree in text form rather than adding a diagram component, preserving the Markdown-only support page structure.
risk: Low; documentation-only addition to the agent-selection index with no route, navigation, or runtime behavior change.

decisions: Expand existing agent-selection scenario and production-readiness articles instead of adding new routes, keeping the 01-24 catalog stable.

Add practical selection guidance for data analysis assistants, multi-agent collaboration, customer-support confidence handling, model-routing algorithms, MCP server operations, enterprise ACL design, and cost/latency budgets.
Cross-link observability, security, fallback, and cost pages so production concerns stay separated by responsibility rather than duplicated in one article.
risk: Low to Medium; documentation-only expansion across eight agent-selection pages, with no route or runtime changes. Main risk is future factual drift for vendor/platform-specific guidance.

decisions: Add an anti-pattern index to the agent-selection landing page so readers can identify overengineering smells before choosing tools.
Map each common failure mode to the underlying design problem and the most relevant agent-selection article instead of adding another standalone page.
Keep the index as a concise Markdown table to preserve the support page structure and avoid navigation changes.
risk: Low; documentation-only index addition with no route, navigation, or runtime behavior change.

decisions: Add a standalone /agent-selection/resources page instead of embedding a large external-link table in the section overview.
Group official links by engineering layer so the page remains a selection aid rather than an unstructured tool bookmark list.
Add the resource page to the agent-selection sidebar and keep the overview page to a single lightweight pointer.
risk: Low; documentation-only resource index and sidebar entry with no runtime behavior change.

decisions: Add Google Analytics through VitePress head configuration so every production page receives the gtag scripts.
Gate analytics injection behind NODE_ENV === production to avoid polluting page view metrics during local development.
Keep the tracking ID in the VitePress config rather than adding a plugin or component, because this is a site-level head concern.
risk: Low; production-only analytics script injection in site config, no content routes or application logic changed.

summary: docs(license): add MIT license for book
decisions: Add a dedicated MIT license file inside docs/book so the VitePress book can be distributed with an explicit license when handled independently from the repository root.
Reuse the repository root MIT license text and copyright notice instead of introducing a different license for the subproject.
risk: Low; license-only addition for the docs/book subproject with no runtime or content behavior change.

decisions: Add IMS AI Copilot background context to the enterprise-agent landing page and reading guide so readers can map the case study to HR, OA, workflow, and knowledge-base systems.
Refine an overly broad enterprise RAG incident claim into a more precise risk statement about unauthorized retrieval entering LLM context.
Connect the data-model and API contract support pages with a minimal implementation loop so the toolkit reads as a coherent delivery path instead of separate reference pages.
Merged session note: 对 enterprise-agent 文章做最小内容质量修正，补 IMS Copilot 背景、收敛一处过强断言，并连接数据模型与 API 契约。
risk: Low; documentation-only quality improvements in the enterprise-agent section with no navigation, route, or runtime changes.
